titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: إِنَّ فِي حِكْمَةِ آلِ دَاوُدَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُسْلِمِ الْعَاقِلِ أَنْ لَا يُرَى ظَاعِناً إِلَّا فِي ثَلَاثٍ مَرَمَّةٍ لِمَعَاشٍ أَوْ تَزَوُّدٍ لِمَعَادٍ أَوْ لَذَّةٍ فِي غَيْرِ ذَاتِ مُحَرَّمٍ

وَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُسْلِمِ الْعَاقِلِ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ سَاعَةٌ يُفْضِي بِهَا إِلَى عَمَلِهِ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ سَاعَةٌ يُلَاقِي إِخْوَانَهُ الَّذِينَ يُفَاوِضُهُمْ وَ يُفَاوِضُونَهُ فِي أَمْرِ آخِرَتِهِ وَ سَاعَةٌ يُخَلِّي بَيْنَ نَفْسِهِ وَ لَذَّاتِهَا فِي غَيْرِ مُحَرَّمٍ فَإِنَّهَا عَوْنٌ عَلَى تِلْكَ السَّاعَتَيْنِ .
arabic.text:
إِنَّ فِي حِكْمَةِ آلِ دَاوُدَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُسْلِمِ الْعَاقِلِ أَنْ لَا يُرَى ظَاعِناً إِلَّا فِي ثَلَاثٍ مَرَمَّةٍ لِمَعَاشٍ أَوْ تَزَوُّدٍ لِمَعَادٍ أَوْ لَذَّةٍ فِي غَيْرِ ذَاتِ مُحَرَّمٍ

وَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُسْلِمِ الْعَاقِلِ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ سَاعَةٌ يُفْضِي بِهَا إِلَى عَمَلِهِ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ سَاعَةٌ يُلَاقِي إِخْوَانَهُ الَّذِينَ يُفَاوِضُهُمْ وَ يُفَاوِضُونَهُ فِي أَمْرِ آخِرَتِهِ وَ سَاعَةٌ يُخَلِّي بَيْنَ نَفْسِهِ وَ لَذَّاتِهَا فِي غَيْرِ مُحَرَّمٍ فَإِنَّهَا عَوْنٌ عَلَى تِلْكَ السَّاعَتَيْنِ .

segments: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from Muhammad Bin Marwan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is in the wisdom of the progeny of Dawood<sup>as</sup>: ‘It is befitting for the intellectual Believer that he does not see the transitory except regarding three (matters) – Restoration of his livelihood, or provision for his Hereafter, or a pleasure in other than with Prohibitions.

And it is befitting for the intellectual Muslim that there happens to be for him a time dedicated to his affairs in what is between him and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, and a time (dedicated to) meeting his brethren whom he advises them and they advise him regarding the matters of the Hereafter, and a time (dedicated) to be alone to himself, and its pleasures in other than the Prohibitions, for these are an aid upon those two times (the first two)’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from Muhammad Bin Marwan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It is in the wisdom of the progeny of Dawood<sup>as</sup>: ‘It is befitting for the intellectual Believer that he does not see the transitory except regarding three (matters) – Restoration of his livelihood, or provision for his Hereafter, or a pleasure in other than with Prohibitions.

And it is befitting for the intellectual Muslim that there happens to be for him a time dedicated to his affairs in what is between him and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, and a time (dedicated to) meeting his brethren whom he advises them and they advise him regarding the matters of the Hereafter, and a time (dedicated) to be alone to himself, and its pleasures in other than the Prohibitions, for these are an aid upon those two times (the first two)’.

segments: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Muhammad ibn Sanaa ‘ah from Muhammad ibn Marwan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is in the wisdom of Ale (family) Dawud that a reasonable Muslim should not travel unless it is for three kinds of tasks: amending one’s means of living, supplies for the hereafter or enjoyment with lawful matters. A reasonable Muslim should assign certain hours of his time to work on the matters between him and Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, certain hours to meet his brothers (in belief) with whom he speaks and they speak with him about the issues of the hereafter, and certain hours for himself and matters of enjoyment with lawful matters which are helpful during these two hours.”
en.sarwar.text: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Muhammad ibn Sanaa ‘ah from Muhammad ibn Marwan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is in the wisdom of Ale (family) Dawud that a reasonable Muslim should not travel unless it is for three kinds of tasks: amending one’s means of living, supplies for the hereafter or enjoyment with lawful matters. A reasonable Muslim should assign certain hours of his time to work on the matters between him and Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, certain hours to meet his brothers (in belief) with whom he speaks and they speak with him about the issues of the hereafter, and certain hours for himself and matters of enjoyment with lawful matters which are helpful during these two hours.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/35)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/35)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مَرْوَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: الْكَمَالُ كُلُّ الْكَمَالِ فِي ثَلَاثَةٍ وَ ذَكَرَ فِي الثَّلَاثَةِ التَّقْدِيرَ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .
arabic.text: الْكَمَالُ كُلُّ الْكَمَالِ فِي ثَلَاثَةٍ وَ ذَكَرَ فِي الثَّلَاثَةِ التَّقْدِيرَ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Rabi’e, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The perfection, every perfection is in three (matters)’, and he<sup>asws</sup> mentioned regarding the third, being the management of the livelihood’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Rabi’e, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The perfection, every perfection is in three (matters)’, and he<sup>asws</sup> mentioned regarding the third, being the management of the livelihood’.

segments: 2. Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Rib’iy from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘All of perfection is in three things.’ He mentioned in the third of the three things to be planning for one’s living.”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Rib’iy from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘All of perfection is in three things.’ He mentioned in the third of the three things to be planning for one’s living.”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ شَاذَانَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ رِبْعِيٍّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: إِصْلَاحُ الْمَالِ مِنَ الْإِيمَانِ .
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Sa’alba and other, from a man,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Correction of the wealth is from the faith’.
en.hubeali.text:
A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Sa’alba and other, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Correction of the wealth is from the faith’.

segments: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from Tha’labah and others from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Keeping one’s wealth in a proper shape is part of belief.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from Tha’labah and others from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Keeping one’s wealth in a proper shape is part of belief.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/36)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/36)

narratorChain.raw: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ ثَعْلَبَةَ وَ غَيْرِهِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَكِيلُ تَمْراً بِيَدِهِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ لَوْ أَمَرْتَ بَعْضَ وُلْدِكَ أَوْ بَعْضَ مَوَالِيكَ فَيَكْفِيَكَ فَقَالَ يَا دَاوُدُ إِنَّهُ لَا يُصْلِحُ الْمَرْءَ الْمُسْلِمَ إِلَّا ثَلَاثَةٌ التَّفَقُّهُ فِي الدِّينِ وَ الصَّبْرُ عَلَى النَّائِبَةِ وَ حُسْنُ التَّقْدِيرِ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .
arabic.text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَكِيلُ تَمْراً بِيَدِهِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ لَوْ أَمَرْتَ بَعْضَ وُلْدِكَ أَوْ بَعْضَ مَوَالِيكَ فَيَكْفِيَكَ فَقَالَ يَا دَاوُدُ إِنَّهُ لَا يُصْلِحُ الْمَرْءَ الْمُسْلِمَ إِلَّا ثَلَاثَةٌ التَّفَقُّهُ فِي الدِّينِ وَ الصَّبْرُ عَلَى النَّائِبَةِ وَ حُسْنُ التَّقْدِيرِ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .

segments: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Dawood Bin Sirham who said,

‘I saw Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> weighing dates with his<sup>asws</sup> hand. So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! If you<sup>asws</sup> could have ordered one of your<sup>asws</sup> sons, or one of your slaves, so they would have sufficed for you<sup>asws</sup>’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Dawood! It is not correct for the Muslim person except three – the pondering in the Religion, and the patience upon the calamities, and improving the management regarding the livelihood’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Dawood Bin Sirham who said,

‘I saw Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> weighing dates with his<sup>asws</sup> hand. So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! If you<sup>asws</sup> could have ordered one of your<sup>asws</sup> sons, or one of your slaves, so they would have sufficed for you<sup>asws</sup>’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Dawood! It is not correct for the Muslim person except three – the pondering in the Religion, and the patience upon the calamities, and improving the management regarding the livelihood’.

segments: 4. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn Faddal from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “I saw abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, measure dates with his own hands; and I said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I wish you would have ordered certain persons of your servants or sons to help you.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘O Dawud, a Muslim cannot achieve a proper condition without three things: proper understanding of religion, exercising patience in hardships, and proper planning for his living and sustenance.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn Faddal from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “I saw abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, measure dates with his own hands; and I said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I wish you would have ordered certain persons of your servants or sons to help you.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘O Dawud, a Muslim cannot achieve a proper condition without three things: proper understanding of religion, exercising patience in hardships, and proper planning for his living and sustenance.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/36)
gradings.label: موثق كالصحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/36)

narratorChain.raw: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ سِرْحَانَ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: إِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِأَهْلِ بَيْتٍ خَيْراً رَزَقَهُمُ الرِّفْقَ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .
arabic.text: إِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِأَهْلِ بَيْتٍ خَيْراً رَزَقَهُمُ الرِّفْقَ فِي الْمَعِيشَةِ .

segments: Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Zareeh Al Muharby,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Intends goodness with a family, He<sup>asws</sup> Sustains them with leniency in the livelihood’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Zareeh Al Muharby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Intends goodness with a family, He<sup>asws</sup> Sustains them with leniency in the livelihood’.

segments: 5. Ali ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah has narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from Muhammad ibn Ali from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Darih al-Muharibiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, wills good for a family He grants them ease in their living conditions.’”
en.sarwar.text: 5. Ali ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah has narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from Muhammad ibn Ali from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Darih al-Muharibiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, wills good for a family He grants them ease in their living conditions.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)
gradings.label: ضعيف
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَبَلَةَ عَنْ ذَرِيحٍ الْمُحَارِبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

parts.text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

titles.ar: باب إِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ وَ تَقْدِيرِ الْمَعِيشَةِ
titles.en: Chapter 12 – Correcting the wealth and managing the livelihood

segments: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَيْكَ بِإِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ فَإِنَّ فِيهِ مَنْبَهَةً لِلْكَرِيمِ وَ اسْتِغْنَاءً عَنِ اللَّئِيمِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَيْكَ بِإِصْلَاحِ الْمَالِ فَإِنَّ فِيهِ مَنْبَهَةً لِلْكَرِيمِ وَ اسْتِغْنَاءً عَنِ اللَّئِيمِ .

segments: From him, from Ahmad, from one of our companions, from Salih Bin Hamza, from one of our companions who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is upon you with the correction of the wealth, for therein is a stimulant for the benevolent and a self-sufficiency from the wicked ones’.
en.hubeali.text:
From him, from Ahmad, from one of our companions, from Salih Bin Hamza, from one of our companions who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is upon you with the correction of the wealth, for therein is a stimulant for the benevolent and a self-sufficiency from the wicked ones’.

segments: 6. It is narrated from him (narrator of previous Fladith) from certain persons of our people from Salih ibn Hamzah from certain persons of our people Hamzah who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You must manage your wealth properly; it increases respect for you and freedom of want from envious ones.’”
en.sarwar.text: 6. It is narrated from him (narrator of previous Fladith) from certain persons of our people from Salih ibn Hamzah from certain persons of our people Hamzah who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You must manage your wealth properly; it increases respect for you and freedom of want from envious ones.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/36)

narratorChain.raw: عَنْهُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا قَالَ
